page: Amazon Web Services r7gd.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server powered by custom AWS Graviton processors. Built on the ARM64 architecture, it features 64 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Neoverse-V1 model running at 2.6 GHz. The processor configuration includes 64 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache per core. The system is equipped with 512.0 GB of RAM, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Local storage is bundled, consisting of 3800 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a 30 Gbps baseline bandwidth managed via the Nitro hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the r7gd.16xlarge demonstrates strong multi-core and memory performance. The stress-ng multi-core CPU test registers a strong score of 97904.32, while the Geekbench multi-core score reaches a top-tier 20690.0. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds at 6200.09 GB/sec and uncached large block write speeds at 116.88 GB/sec. Database operations are highly performant, as evidenced by a Passmark database score of 31336.22 and Redis SET operations processing 101.36 million operations per second. However, single-core performance remains average, with a stress-ng single-core score of 1532.98, and text generation for small LLM models is relatively poor at 9.82 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture and high resource density of the r7gd.16xlarge provide a favorable balance of memory capacity and local NVMe storage without the overhead of x86 licensing. The dedicated physical cores and high memory bandwidth make this instance well-suited for memory-intensive workloads, large-scale database deployments, caching layers, and software compilation tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.16xlarge is a memory-optimized, ARM64-based server powered by 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz. It features 512.0 GB of RAM, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 30 Gbps. Benchmark results indicate top-tier memory performance, with a Passmark memory score of 3160.9 and cached read-only bandwidth of 6200.09 GB/sec. Multi-core capabilities are strong, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 20690.0 and software compilation score of 58508.0. While single-core performance is average, the high memory density and fast local NVMe storage make this server highly effective for database operations, Redis caching, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server featuring 64 Graviton cores, 512 GB RAM, and 3800 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, designed for high-throughput database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r7gd.16xlarge is an ARM64 memory-optimized server with 64 Graviton cores, 512 GB RAM, and 3800 GB local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: An ARM64 memory-optimized platform combining dedicated physical cores with high-speed local NVMe storage for demanding database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARM64 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz.
512.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio.
3800 GB of high-speed local NVMe SSD instance storage.
30 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core software compilation performance.
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and memory-intensive applications.
